purpose: Measure the CORE FRACTION of Rapi: Red Hood's projectile-EXPLOSION instances (the ~1.6-4.5M popup class), i.e. what fraction render with the red 'CORE HIT' label vs plain white / orange (crit). Answers recording-wishlist #1 as far as EXISTING footage allows.
cross_ref: docs/probes/control + carry/rrh control.MP4 (prior FB1 read, rrh-fb1-reread.json)
method: ffmpeg native-res frame extraction (60fps in FB windows) + upscaled tight crops of the popup column; OCR-by-eye of individual 7-digit popups and their colour/label. Colour convention (owner-confirmed): red 'CORE HIT' text = core; orange number (+ crit star) = crit; white plain = neither. A hit can be core AND crit (orange number carrying a red CORE HIT label).

video.focus_unit: Rapi: Red Hood (rapi-red-hood); per owner ALL rendered popups are hers

fb_windows_video_s.note: FULL BURST splashes at ~t=14.5, 33.0, 51.0; period ~18s; focus unit -> heavy gauge, near back-to-back bursts

headline_finding.explosions_core: CONFIRMED. Her projectile-explosion instances DO land as core: multiple clean red 'CORE HIT' labels on 7-digit explosion bodies (5,728,232 in FB1 AND FB3; ~7,948,092 in FB2). This is NOT the small out-of-burst ATTACH class (which never cores) - these are the in-burst detonation bodies.
headline_finding.explosions_also_miss_core: CONFIRMED. The SAME explosion class also lands non-core: plain WHITE bodies (1,600,449 / 4,414,404) and ORANGE crit-but-non-core bodies (3,350,040 / 6,621,606). So the core rate is neither 0 nor 100%.
headline_finding.core_fraction_estimate: ~1/3 (point est 0.30-0.45). The plain WHITE (non-core) body is the most persistent/dominant popup in every burst; red 'CORE HIT' bodies are clearly present but the minority. Explicitly NOT near-100% (would be the isolated-shard error).
headline_finding.confidence: LOW-MEDIUM on the exact fraction; HIGH on the qualitative (explosions core partially, not fully).
headline_finding.why_not_precise: Explosion popups overlap heavily with the dense normal-core column AND with each other; crit (orange), core (red) and MULTIPLE projectile sub-hit coefficients all coexist and confound a clean value-based instance count. This is exactly the overlap the wishlist flagged - a truly clean count still needs the ISOLATED single-explosion-batch slow-mo recording.

explosion_instance_reads.note: orange = crit, no CORE HIT label
clear red 'CORE HIT' text directly above 5728232; mid-burst rocket detonation
the persistent/dominant FB2 body; often TWO stacked simultaneously; == prior finder's ~4.5M 'clean explosion body'
= 4,414,404 x1.500 exactly -> confirms crit step x1.5 and that this is the SAME base rolling crit
= 4,414,404 x1.80; carries its own red 'CORE HIT' label. Orange tint suggests core+crit; either way CORED.
smaller red CORE-HIT body (lower-coefficient sub-hit or partial read); labelled CORE
plain body, coexists with the 5,728,232 core body
red 'CORE HIT'; SAME value as FB1 t=16.3 (buff plateau matched)

tally.distinct_value_ratio: 4 core-labelled / 9 total distinct reads = 0.44, BUT weighting by on-screen persistence/frequency the WHITE non-core body dominates -> weighted core fraction pulls DOWN to ~0.30-0.35.

multiplier_notes.core_step_observed: ~x1.8 (7,948,092 / 4,414,404 = 1.80) in FB2; FB3 normals showed core = x2.0 (99,677 / 49,839 = 2.000). Core multiplier is BUFFED above the base ~1.5 (Crown/comp core-damage buffs).
multiplier_notes.prior_x1.667_claim: NOT reproduced. The prior u7 read reported a clean x1.667 core / x2.0 core+crit off a 4,562,076 body. I found the fully-ramped body at 4,414,404 (~3% off 4,562,076) but the core step here is ~1.8-2.0, not 1.667. Attribute to buffed core damage / different ramp instant; does not change the qualitative 'explosions core' finding.

per_explosion_base_value.detail: FB1/FB3 resolved a ~1.60M plain body; FB2 resolved a ~4.41M plain body. Non-monotonic across FBs -> these are DIFFERENT explosion sub-hit coefficients (RRH's projectile majors span ~{1.0..1.675}), not a single ramping body. The ~4.4M matches the prior finder's ~4.5M largest body.

range_independence_check.observed: The core/non-core MIX looked roughly consistent across FB1, FB2 and FB3 (each burst showed both white bodies and CORE-HIT bodies in similar proportion). No wild swing.
range_independence_check.interpretation: Consistent with the task's prediction that attached explosions detonate ON the boss body and are range-INDEPENDENT - the explosion core rate did not obviously track boss distance. Confidence low (proportions eyeballed, not exhaustively counted).

structure_ambiguity.note: Could not cleanly separate two hypotheses: (a) ONE explosion hit per rocket that independently rolls core/crit at a ~1/3 rate, vs (b) a MULTI-hit explosion with a fixed set of sub-hits where SOME sub-hits always core and others never (making ~1/3 a structural constant). Evidence leans (b): a white body and a CORE-HIT body persistently COEXIST within the same detonation window (FB2 4,414,404 white + 7,948,092 CORE HIT together; FB3 1,600,445 white + 5,728,232 CORE HIT together). Either way the actionable modelling input is the same: ~1/3 of explosion instance-damage cores.
structure_ambiguity.modelling_implication: For the engine reopen (add core to her ~coreable projectile damage): apply core to roughly ONE-THIRD of the explosion instances/damage, NOT all of it. Full-core would over-credit.

discipline: Measurement only. No engine/sim/override edits. Values are OCR-by-eye from frame-stepped native-resolution upscaled crops. Scratch frames were discarded after reading.
